text: Effective team management requires a careful balance of skills. Clear communication ensures that everyone understands their role and the project's goals. A good leader delegates tasks based on each person's strengths, rather than doing everything alone. This builds trust and efficiency. Regular, honest feedback helps team members improve and feel valued. When problems arise, it's important to address conflict resolution early, by listening to different viewpoints and finding a fair solution. Finally, to maintain motivation, a leader should recognize achievements and create a positive work environment where people feel supported and inspired to do their best.
